# slowdep

> Wrap any async function with realistic, lognormal latency. Test how your app survives slow, unpredictable dependencies.

## What it does

slowdep is a zero-dependency Node.js/TypeScript library that replaces flat `setTimeout` delays with latency sampled from a lognormal distribution — the same statistical shape real production dependencies produce. Fast most of the time, occasionally slow, rarely very slow.

Use it to stress-test retry logic, timeouts, and circuit breakers without needing a degraded service or slow network.

## Install

```
npm install slowdep
```

## API

### withLatency(fn, preset | options)

Wraps a single async function. Returns a new function with the same signature.

```ts
import { withLatency } from 'slowdep';

// named preset
const slowQuery = withLatency(db.query, 'postgres');

// custom profile
const slowFetch = withLatency(fetchAPI, {
  p50: 100,        // median latency in ms
  p99: 2000,       // 99th-percentile latency in ms
  errorRate: 0.01, // fraction of calls that throw a transient error (0–1)
});
```

### withLatencyAll(client, preset | options)

Wraps every async method on an object (e.g. a database client).

```ts
import { withLatencyAll } from 'slowdep';

const slowRedis = withLatencyAll(redisClient, 'redis');
await slowRedis.get('key');
await slowRedis.set('k', 'v');
```

### Options object

| Field       | Type   | Description                                      |
|-------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------|
| p50         | number | Median latency in milliseconds                   |
| p99         | number | 99th-percentile latency in milliseconds          |
| errorRate   | number | Fraction of calls that reject (default: 0)       |

## Built-in presets

| Preset      | p50    | p95     | p99     | Error rate |
|-------------|--------|---------|---------|------------|
| postgres    | 5ms    | 50ms    | 200ms   | 0.1%       |
| mysql       | 4ms    | 40ms    | 180ms   | 0.1%       |
| redis       | 1ms    | 5ms     | 20ms    | 0.05%      |
| mongodb     | 8ms    | 60ms    | 250ms   | 0.1%       |
| dynamodb    | 3ms    | 15ms    | 50ms    | 0.05%      |
| s3          | 30ms   | 150ms   | 500ms   | 0.1%       |
| stripe      | 200ms  | 800ms   | 2000ms  | 0.2%       |
| openai      | 800ms  | 3000ms  | 8000ms  | 0.5%       |
| anthropic   | 600ms  | 2500ms  | 7000ms  | 0.5%       |
| http        | 80ms   | 300ms   | 1000ms  | 1%         |

## How the math works

slowdep fits a lognormal distribution to the p50 and p99 values using the Box-Muller transform:

```
mu    = ln(p50)
sigma = (ln(p99) - mu) / 2.326   // 2.326 = z-score for 99th percentile
delay = exp(mu + sigma * Z)       // Z ~ N(0,1)
```

The sampled delay is capped at `p99 * 3` to prevent runaway outliers.

## TypeScript

Full TypeScript types are included. No `@types/` package needed.

```ts
import { withLatency, withLatencyAll } from 'slowdep';
import type { LatencyOptions, LatencyPreset } from 'slowdep';
```
